validate.notes: BLOCKED: harness sandbox is broken in this session. Every Bash invocation returns 'EACCES: permission denied, mkdir /home/worker/.claude/session-env', so docker build / NOP test / gold test / rubric judge cannot be run. Additionally Edit/Write to environment/Dockerfile, solution/solve.sh, and tests/test_outputs.py return EACCES (only status.json and a couple of placeholder .txt files are writable here), so even the file-level fixes I identified cannot be applied. By inspection, the suspected regression is solve.sh:7 - the early-return grep for 'requesting_frame->GetLastCommittedOrigin().GetURL()' would match content already present at base commit in RequestPermission (per the prior validate node's notes), causing the patch to be skipped on a fresh container. Recommended fix once filesystem is writable: change that check to grep for the post-patch CheckSerialAccessPermission(content::RenderFrameHost*) signature in shell/browser/web_contents_permission_helper.h. Prior history shows validate has passed before (2026-04-22 minimax run, gold=1.0/nop=0.0).

previous_validate.notes: Fixed solve.sh check pattern: previous check 'requesting_frame->GetLastCommittedOrigin().GetURL()' was matching line 222 in RequestPermission (which already had this in base commit). New check looks for 'content::RenderFrameHost* requesting_frame' in CheckPermission function signature. All 21 tests pass with gold fix.
